The National Library on Money & Politics searches databases for you

Richard Mullins Researcher and system administrator for the Library October 1992
Data & DatabasesGovernment & PoliticsJournalism & Newsroom

This piece highlights the National Library on Money & Politics, which helps journalists access data on political contributions. The library has bought FEC tapes dating back to 1979, offering tailored ...

This piece highlights the National Library on Money & Politics, which helps journalists access data on political contributions. The library has bought FEC tapes dating back to 1979, offering tailored data for states, districts, and officials, reducing the burden on news organizations. Common data requests include PAC contributions and funding from specific interests. The library assists reporters by providing processed data in formats like CSV or dBase, enhancing campaign finance reporting.

Collaborating with the Center for Responsive Politics, the library aids reporters in understanding and utilizing this crucial data efficiently.
